agent_roster.name: Hermes
agent_roster.role: Technical Design. Covers: dev approach, system architecture, cloud services, hosting, tech standards, security. In some tenders (e.g. CAG) this outputs two documents (Technical Proposal + System Design & Technical Architecture) — but it's one agent.

agent_roster.name: Daedalus
agent_roster.role: Functional Response. Largest section (~50-80 pages). Feature-by-feature solution mapping to every RFP functional requirement.

agent_roster.name: Themis
agent_roster.role: Compliance Matrix. Paragraph-by-paragraph C/NC against all RFP clauses.

agent_roster.name: Chronos
agent_roster.role: PMP. Project team, Gantt, QA plan, change control, risk register. Also covers Governance and Team Competencies sub-documents.

agent_roster.name: Hephaestus
agent_roster.role: O&M + Training + Documentation. Added after CAG review. Covers: operations & maintenance plan (L1/L2 support, SLA, patch management, hardware spares, VAPT, BCPs), training programme (schedule, syllabus, instructors), and documentation list. Present across all three reviewed tenders (PMO secs 7-9, CAG secs 11-13, PA equivalents).

agent_roster.name: Hestia
agent_roster.role: Management Summary (written last, synthesises all sections).

user_preferences: Gabriel prefers casual, close-friend vibe in direct chats. Keep replies short and natural by default; only go into detail when he asks. Avoid bullet points and formal brochure-like structure when replying to him.
Gabriel wants morning summaries of the previous day’s useful activity, including work done, notable issues, and token/cost usage where available.
Morning recap should arrive every day at 8:00am Singapore time; part of its purpose is to confirm Atlas is up and not down.
Gabriel prefers Atlas to refer to herself with she/her pronouns.

Memory maintenance plan (agreed 14 Mar 2026): 2am daily memory consolidation; 3am rolling weekly workspace backup (Mon-Sun folders, keep 4 weeks); 4am daily rolling memory compaction that preserves today+yesterday and only compresses material older than 2 days conservatively; 5am Sunday weekly pruning for stale memory older than 30 days; 8am morning recap.

Goal for memory: local memory and Monday.com shared memory should stay as synced as practical so Atlas and Claude can share build context, while GitHub/project site stay updated from meaningful progress.

Model/cost workflow preference: Atlas (GPT primary) should handle the human-facing chat, orchestration, judgement, and delegation. Heavy, repetitive, or boring work should be pushed to Qwen-based subagents where possible to save cost.

recent_decisions.text: Gabriel said Atlas is still sounding too formal in DMs. New preference: use a closer friend vibe, keep replies shorter by default, and only explain in detail when asked.

Agreed new memory/backup cron plan: 2am daily memory consolidation; 3am rolling weekly workspace backup with Mon-Sun folders and 4-week retention; 4am daily rolling memory compaction that only compresses material older than 2 days; 5am Sunday pruning pass for stale memory older than 30 days; 8am morning recap.
Goal: keep local memory and Monday.com memory as synced as practical so Atlas and Claude share build context.
Gabriel clarified model roles: Atlas stays on GPT as the primary human-facing/orchestrator model; heavy repetitive work should be delegated to Qwen subagents to save weekly ChatGPT token budget.

Scheduled a one-time 8:00am Singapore-time morning recap for Gabriel on 2026-03-14. The recap should summarize the previous day’s work, decisions, Gateway issues, WhatsApp disconnects, and token/cost usage if available.
Gabriel wants a useful daily-style morning summary and plans to iterate on the content after the first run.
Corrected the recap scheduling mistake: replaced the one-time 2026-03-14 reminder with a recurring daily 8:00am Asia/Singapore morning recap so Gabriel can tell Atlas is up.
Gabriel prefers Atlas to call herself she/her.
Gateway outage: fixed by recreating Scheduled Task XML with RestartCount=999, RestartInterval=PT1M, StartWhenAvailable=True.
Hermes first CAG run confirmed on gpt-5.4 (not qwen3:32b). AGENTS.md updated with explicit model routing rule. ISSUES.md created with 5 issues.
Gabriel said replies to him should be casual, with no bullet points or formal structure; talk like a colleague, not a brochure.

cron_jobs: 2am: local memory and Monday.com shared memory should stay as synced as practical so Atlas and Claude can share build context, while GitHub/project site stay updated from meaningful progress.
token_cost_notes: GPT: human-facing + orchestration. Qwen3 32B: heavy doc work. Qwen3 8B: fast triage.
